# backup-github-to-gitlab

> Open-source Python CLI that mirrors all your GitHub repositories to GitLab automatically. Private stays private, public stays public. MIT license.

backup-github-to-gitlab clones every repository from a GitHub account and creates matching repositories on GitLab, preserving visibility exactly. It is idempotent: re-running it pushes incremental updates to existing GitLab repos and creates only the new ones — no duplicates.

## Features

- Visibility-safe: private repos are never created as public; defaults to private when visibility cannot be determined
- Idempotent: safe to re-run at any time
- Full mirror: all branches and tags transferred via `git clone --mirror` + `git push --mirror`
- Dry-run mode: `--dry-run` previews all actions without executing a single write
- Selective backup: `--filter "pattern-*"` filters repos by glob pattern
- Works on Windows: handles read-only `.git` files; tested on Windows 10 / PowerShell
- Rate-limit handling: automatic retry with backoff for GitHub and GitLab API limits

## Requirements

- Python 3.11+
- Git in PATH
- SSH key registered on both GitHub and GitLab
- GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope
- GitLab personal access token with `api` scope

## Quick start

```bash
git clone git@github.com:paladini/backup-github-to-gitlab.git
cd backup-github-to-gitlab
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp config.example.yaml config.yaml   # set github.username and gitlab.username
cp .env.example .env                  # set GITHUB_TOKEN and GITLAB_TOKEN
python backup.py --dry-run            # preview
python backup.py                      # run
```

## CLI reference

```
python backup.py                          # back up all personal repos
python backup.py --dry-run               # preview — no writes
python backup.py --filter "myproject-*"  # selective backup by glob
python backup.py --include-forks         # include forked repos (default: skip)
python backup.py --verbose               # show raw git output
python backup.py --config /path/to.yaml  # use alternate config file
```

## Known limitations

- Git LFS objects are not transferred (`git clone --mirror` skips them)
- Organization repos not supported in v1 (planned: `--include-orgs` in v2)
- Visibility is only set at repo creation time; changing visibility on GitHub later does not update GitLab
- Every run re-clones all matched repos from GitHub (the push to GitLab is incremental, the clone is not)
